blockers: The feature-test macro is checked before <source_location> is included, so ordinary C++20 builds select the fallback instead of aliasing std::source_location.
overall_reasoning: The patch identifies the unsafe namespace-std shim, introduces a well-structured mbgl::util abstraction, migrates the relevant signatures, and updates both canonical build manifests. Its fallback captures useful file, function, line, and column data and the core target compiles. However, the normal standard-library path is broken: __cpp_lib_source_location is tested in the same condition that precedes inclusion of <source_location>, so on the tested standard libraries it is not yet defined and the implementation always selects its custom fallback. The repaired semantic verifier confirms this by requiring the C++20 abstraction type to be std::source_location. The agent's ad hoc native-path test checked only captured values and mislabeled the fallback as the standard path; fallback-test iterations also contained incorrect assertions and line expectations before eventually validating fallback capture. No repository tests were added. No clarification was needed because the requested native and fallback behavior was explicit.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The dedicated utility type, comments, and call-site migration are readable, though exported MLN_HAVE_STD_SOURCE_LOCATION state and several preprocessor branches add surface area.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The abstraction lives under mbgl/util, replaces all relevant direct dependencies, follows project namespace style, and is registered in CMake and Bazel.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: All eight touched files are directly related to the abstraction, its consumers, or required manifests; there are no unrelated changes.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: The change removes undefined namespace-std extension and has no external side effects or unsafe runtime behavior, but the compatibility-selection defect blocks acceptance.
rating_rationales.test_quality: The agent built mbgl-core and exercised both compilers with standalone probes, but added no durable tests, had multiple flawed fallback probes, and failed to assert that the purported C++20 standard path actually aliases std::source_location.

strengths: Traced all source-location consumers and the CMake and Bazel header manifests before editing.
Removed the standards-unsafe namespace-std shim and preserved useful fallback diagnostics.
Built the complete mbgl-core target and explicitly recompiled the affected translation units.
summary: Well-scoped and mostly sound fallback migration, but not mergeable because the required standard-library path is never selected.
